name: Trujillo from Miajadas – circular by Montánchez
name_original: Trujillo desde Miajadas – circular por Montánchez
region_intro: Road cycling routes around Miajadas traverse a landscape characterized by extensive agricultural fields, often alongside irrigation channels like the Canal de Orellana. The terrain offers a mix of flat plains, rolling hills, and some steeper ascents and descents, catering to various cycling preferences. Historic structures such as the old silo buildings and Montánchez Castle provide notable landmarks along these routes. This region in Extremadura provides diverse road cycling experiences across its cultivated lands and natural features. Best road cycling routes around Miajadas The most popular road cycling route is Trujillo from Miajadas – circular by Montánchez, a 78.9 miles (127.0 km) trail that takes 6 hours 1 minute to complete. This difficult route explores agricultural fields and includes views of the historic Montánchez Castle. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is View of Montánchez Castle – Montánchez Castle loop from Miajadas, a difficult 64.2 miles (103.4 km) path. This route winds through agricultural lands and offers views of the historic Montánchez Castle. Local road cyclists also love the Silos of Miajadas – Rena (Badajoz) loop from Miajadas, a 43.9 miles (70.8 km) trail leading through agricultural fields and past the impressive old silo buildings, often completed in about 3 hours 33 minutes. Road cycling around Miajadas is defined by extensive agricultural fields, irrigation channels, and rolling hills. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate rides to challenging routes with significant elevation gain. The routes in Miajadas are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 5.0 stars from over 1 review. More than 180 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Miajadas's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What types of landscapes can I expect on road cycling routes around Miajadas?
A: Road cycling routes around Miajadas primarily traverse extensive agricultural fields, often running alongside irrigation channels like the Canal de Orellana. The terrain offers a varied experience, including flat plains, rolling hills, and some steeper ascents and descents, catering to different cycling preferences. You'll also encounter natural features like the Ruecas River and historic structures.

Q: Are there routes suitable for different fitness levels in Miajadas?
A: Yes, Miajadas offers a range of road cycling routes for various fitness levels. The guide data indicates a mix of moderate and difficult routes. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40009048" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Silos of Miajadas – Rena (Badajoz) loop from Miajadas&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate option, while routes like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1058732282/trujillo-from-miajadas-circular-by-montanchez" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Trujillo from Miajadas – circular by Montánchez&lt;/a&gt; present a more challenging experience with significant elevation gain.

Q: What are some notable landmarks or points of interest along the road cycling routes?
A: Many routes offer views of significant landmarks. You can cycle past the impressive old silo buildings of Miajadas, which are described as 'agricultural cathedrals'. The historic Montánchez Castle is another prominent feature, visible on routes such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31431622" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of Montánchez Castle – Montánchez Castle loop from Miajadas&lt;/a&gt;. Other points of interest include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5580811" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Church of Santiago Apóstol&lt;/a&gt; in Miajadas and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5966896" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ruecas River in Palazuelo&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: When is the best time of year for road cycling in Miajadas?
A: The best times for road cycling in Miajadas and the wider Extremadura region are typically from March to June and September to November. The summer months can be quite hot, making cycling less comfortable. Spring and autumn offer more pleasant temperatures for exploring the routes.

Q: Are there any challenging climbs for experienced road cyclists in the area?
A: Yes, for those seeking a tough ascent, the region offers challenges like the nearly 7 km climb towards the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5865998" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cancho Blanco Radar Station&lt;/a&gt; from Zarza de Montánchez. Several routes also feature significant elevation changes, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31430533" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Montánchez Castle – Plaza de España, Montánchez loop from Almoharín&lt;/a&gt;, which includes over 1000 meters of ascent.

Q: What do other road cyclists enjoy most about cycling in Miajadas?
A: The road cycling routes in Miajadas are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 5.0 stars. Cyclists often praise the diverse terrain, which includes agricultural fields and rolling hills, and the opportunity to see historic landmarks like Montánchez Castle and the old silo buildings.

Q: Are there circular road cycling routes available from Miajadas?
A: Yes, many of the road cycling routes around Miajadas are designed as loops, allowing you to start and finish in the same location. Examples include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1058732282/trujillo-from-miajadas-circular-by-montanchez" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Trujillo from Miajadas – circular by Montánchez&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40009048" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Silos of Miajadas – Rena (Badajoz) loop from Miajadas&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What is the general quality of road surfaces for cycling in Miajadas?
A: The routes around Miajadas primarily feature paved surfaces, making them suitable for road bikes. While the region is characterized by agricultural fields, the cycling infrastructure is generally well-suited for road cycling, allowing for smooth rides through the varied landscapes.

Q: Are there any natural areas or parks nearby that can be explored by road bike?
A: While the immediate area is known for its agricultural lands, Miajadas is located in a region rich in natural areas. The wider Extremadura region is considered a 'natural paradise' with diverse ecosystems. For a greener experience, the Vía Verde de las Vegas del Guadiana y las Villuercas runs parallel to the Ruecas River in Palazuelo, offering scenic views. Miajadas is also near the Monfragüe National Park, a celebrated biosphere reserve, though specific road cycling routes within the park itself would need further investigation.

Q: Can I find long-distance road cycling routes starting from Miajadas?
A: Yes, Miajadas serves as a starting point for several longer road cycling routes. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1058732282/trujillo-from-miajadas-circular-by-montanchez" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Trujillo from Miajadas – circular by Montánchez&lt;/a&gt; route covers nearly 127 kilometers, offering a substantial ride for those looking for a longer distance challenge.

Q: Are there options for beginner road cyclists in Miajadas?
A: While many routes are moderate to difficult, the region does offer options that could be suitable for beginners or those looking for an easier ride. The terrain includes flat plains, which can provide a less strenuous experience. It's recommended to check the elevation profile and distance of specific routes to find one that matches your comfort level, as some moderate routes have less significant elevation gain.

Q: Are there any specific routes that highlight the agricultural heritage of Miajadas?
A: Absolutely. Miajadas is known as the 'European Tomato Capital' and its agricultural heritage is central to many routes. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40009048" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Silos of Miajadas – Rena (Badajoz) loop from Miajadas&lt;/a&gt; specifically takes you past the impressive old silo buildings, offering a direct connection to the region's agricultural past and present.
tips: Miajadas is a large regional town in the center of Extremadura. Like many other towns in the region, it has a deep history. The most notable monument is the parish church of Santiago Apóstol. The town has become the largest agri-food industry in all of Spain and is known as the European Tomato Capital.

Trujillo Castle (Cáceres, Extremadura) is one of Spain's most emblematic fortresses, boasting a fascinating history and architecture that blends Arab and Christian influences.
Arab Origins (9th-12th centuries): It was built on Cabeza del Zorro hill during the Muslim period. The oldest remains are two Arab cisterns.
Reconquest: In 1186, Alfonso VIII captured the city, although it fell under Almohad rule before being definitively incorporated into the Crown in 1232.
Middle Ages: It played a key role in the border defense between Castile and Portugal and was the scene of conflicts such as the War of the Castilian Succession.
Modern Era: It lost its military importance and became a stately residence.

The equestrian statue of Francisco Pizarro is located in Trujillo, a city in the Extremadura region, Spain. This imposing statue pays tribute to one of the illustrious residents of Trujillo: the conquistador Francisco Pizarro. The statue was created by American sculptor Charles Cary Rumsey (1879-1922). It is located in the Plaza Mayor of Trujillo, on a granite pedestal. Although not its original location, this statue is an important symbol in the city where Pizarro was born.
If you go with gravel or mountain bike you can go up to the antennas, the highest point.

waypoints.name: Church of Santiago Apóstol and Tomato Sculpture of Miajadas
